key_insight: Equivariant graph neural networks better capture 3D geometry, enabling more accurate optical spectrum prediction for materials than rotation-invariant surrogate models.
engineering_relevance: This improves high-throughput screening of optoelectronic materials by speeding identification of candidates with desirable optical properties for devices like solar cells.
method: Adapted the equivariant GNN GotenNet for optical spectra prediction.
Evaluated the model on multiple datasets, including 10,533 structures with RPA-computed spectra.
Compared performance against current state-of-the-art surrogate models, with gains strongest in the 0–8 eV range.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports testing on multiple datasets and highlights a recently published set of 10,533 structures with RPA-level spectra, where the proposed model outperformed prior methods, especially for low-energy spectra and static real permittivity.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on access to high-quality training spectra and careful handling of computational cost when scaling equivariant models.
paper_title: Equivariant Graph Neural Networks Improve Optical Spectra Prediction for Materials Screening
